data.title: Watsco misses Q3 estimates
data.description:
• Watsco press release (NYSE:WSO): Q3 GAAP EPS of $4.22 misses by $0.52.
  • Revenue of $2.16B misses by $80M.

data.title: Winnebago slides after earnings reflect sluggish retail demand
data.description:
Winnebago Industries (NYSE:WGO) reported a 6.5% decline in revenue in FQ4 to $720.9 million. The revenue decrease reflected the sluggish retail demand environment and operating inefficiencies within the Winnebago branded businesses. Revenue in the towable RV segment was down 7.2%, while revenue in the motorhome RV segment was down 3.1%.

CEO Michael Happe said the RV industry continues to face various headwinds, including uncertain retail conditions, higher inventory carrying costs and slightly elevated inventories in the motorhome segment, leading to continued dealer hesitancy and increased promotional efforts. "Despite the challenging operating conditions, in the fourth quarter we continued to focus on the variables within our control. We prioritized profitability and cash generation, aggressively managed inventory, and invested in new products and technologies to drive future growth," he highlighted.

Gross profit margin decreased 340 basis points during the quarter to 13.1%, reflecting higher warranty expense, operational challenges, and deleverage. Winnebago (WGO) saw an operating loss of $17.8 million vs. operating income of $57.5 million in FQ4 last year. Consolidate adjusted EBITDA was down 60% year-over-year to $28.7 million, vs. $48.9 million consensus. EPS came in at $0.28 vs. $1.41 a year ago.

Looking ahead, Winnebago Industries (WGO) anticipates total North American RV wholesale shipments in the range of 320,000 to 350,000 units. Based on that outlook and the current business environment, the company expects FY25 consolidated revenues in the range of $2.9 billion to $3.2 billion, reported earnings per diluted share of $2.40 to $3.90, and adjusted earnings per diluted share of $3.00 to $4.50. WGO's outlook considers prevailing trends in the RV sector, including competitive dynamics, shifts in consumer preferences, and key macroeconomic factors that may influence overall demand.

Shares of Winnebago (WGO) fell 7.78% in premarket trading on Wednesday. Recreation vehicle stocks in general are mixed in the premarket session as well, including Lazydays Holdings (GORV) +2.2%, Thor Industries (THO) -2.4%, LCI Industries (LCII) -0.2%, Camping World (CWH) -2.6%, Malibu Boats (MBUU) +0.1%, MasterCraft Boat Holdings (MCFT) -0.3%.

data.title: NextEra Energy Partners GAAP EPS of -$0.43, revenue of $319M misses by $22.17M
data.description:
• NextEra Energy Partners press release (NYSE:NEP): Q3 EPS of -$0.43 may not be comparable to $0.56.
  • Revenue of $319M (+3.6% Y/Y) misses by $22.17M.
  • NextEra Energy Partners continues to expect the run-rate contribution for adjusted EBITDA from itsforecasted portfolio at Dec. 31, 2024, to be in the range of $1.9 billion to $2.1 billion. The year-end 2024run-rate projection reflects expected calendar-year 2025 contributions from the forecasted portfolio atyear-end 2024.

data.title: Kaixin Holdings to execute 1-for-60 share consolidation
data.description:
• Kaixin Holdings (NASDAQ:KXIN) is set to implement a 1-for-60 share consolidation of its ordinary shares on October 25, 2024.
  • As a result of the consolidation, every sixty shares of the company’s ordinary shares will be automatically consolidated into one ordinary share. The ordinary shares are expected to begin trading on a post-consolidation basis at market open on October 25.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: General Dynamics missed estimates in Q3, Aerospace segment sales +22.1% Y/Y
Lixte Biotechnology files to sell units, no amount given
data.description:
• Lixte Biotechnology (NASDAQ:LIXT) filed a prospectus related to the sale of units for an undisclosed amount.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities.

data.title: Vicinity Motor gets delisting notice from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Vicinity Motor (NASDAQ:VEV) said it has received a letter from Nasdaq, stating that the company has failed to meet the minimum bid price requirement.
  • The firm also said it has received a separate delisting determination based on its recent entry into receivership pursuant to the Order of the Supreme Court of British Columbia.
  • The company said it will not appeal the decision.

data.title: Travel + Leisure points to momentum with the vacation ownership business
data.description:
Travel + Leisure Co. (NYSE:TNL) reported revenue increased 0.7% year-over-year in Q3 to $993 million.

Vacation Ownership segment revenue increased 2% to $825 million in the quarter compared to the same period a year ago. Net vacation ownership interest sales increased 5% year over year, despite a higher provision rate and gross VOI sales increased 1%, despite lower fee-for-service sales.

Travel and Membership segment revenue decreased 3% to $168 million compared to the same period in the prior year. The decline was driven by an 8% decrease in transactions, partially offset by a 3% increase in revenue per transaction.

On the balance sheet, Travel + Leisure (TNL) ended the quarter with a leverage ratio for covenant purposes of 3.4X. The timeshare company had $3.5 billion of corporate debt outstanding at the end of the quarter, which excluded $2.0 billion of non-recourse debt related to its securitized notes receivables portfolio.

"We have good momentum in our Vacation Ownership business and were especially pleased with our VPG performance, which remains consistently above $3,000, even during our peak new owner mix quarters," stated CEO Michael Brown.

Looking ahead, Travel + Leisure Co. (TNL) expects the momentum in the Vacation Ownership business to continue after achieved its targeted new owner mix, the ramping up of Accor sales, and easing of interest rate headwinds. The company also expects further progress on the Travel & Membership transformation to allow that segment to stabilize. TNL guided for Q4 adjusted EBITDA of $240 million to $260 million and full-year adjusted EBITDA of $915 million to $935 million.

Shares of Travel + Leisure (TNL) were up 1.0% in premarket trading on Wednesday.

data.title: Key takeaways from Coca-Cola's earnings report
data.description:
Coca-Cola Company (NYSE:KO) moved higher in early trading on Wednesday after edging past organic sales estimates with its Q3 earnings report and setting solid guidance. Revenue dipped 0.8% year-over-year to $11.9 billion for the quarter that ended on September 27, while EPS slotted in at $0.77 vs. $0.75 consensus. "Our business continues to demonstrate resilience in the face of a dynamic external environment," stated CEO James Quincey.

Organic sales shine again

Organic revenue was up 9% during the quarter vs. +6.3% consensus estimate, led by gains in the Latin America (+24%), and North America (+12%). The organic sales growth mark was notably higher than PepsiCo's (PEP) 1.3% mark for a roughly overlapping period. Unit case volume was sown 1% for Coca-Cola (KO) during the quarter. Growth led by Brazil, the Philippines and Japan was more than offset by declines in China, Mexico and Türkiye. Price/mix accounted for ten percentage points of growth. Approximately 4 points were driven by pricing from markets experiencing intense inflation, with the remainder driven by pricing actions in the marketplace and favorable mix. Concentrate sales were 1 point behind unit case volume, primarily due to the timing of concentrate shipments.

North America outperforms

Unit case volume in North America was even as growth in Trademark Coca-Cola, juice, value-added dairy and plant-based beverages and sparkling flavors was offset by a decline in water, sports, coffee and tea. Price/mix grew 11%, driven by favorable mix and pricing actions in the marketplace. Concentrate sales were 1 point ahead of unit case volume, primarily due to the timing of concentrate shipments. Operating income in North America grew 10%, which included items impacting comparability. Comparable currency neutral operating income grew 16%, primarily driven by strong organic revenue growth, partially offset by higher input costs and an increase in marketing investments.

Margins march higher

Consolidate comparable operating margin (non-GAAP) was 30.7% versus 29.7% in the prior year. The operating margin improvement was primarily driven by strong organic revenue growth and the impact of refranchising bottling operations, partially offset by currency headwinds. Comparable operating margin expansion was primarily driven by strong organic revenue (non-GAAP) growth and the impact of refranchising bottling operations, partially offset by currency headwinds.

Looking ahead

Looking ahead, Coca-Cola (KO) sees full-year adjusted organic revenue growth of +10% vs. +9.9% consensus. The company expects to deliver comparable currency neutral EPS (non-GAAP) growth of 14% to 15%. Shares of Coca-Cola (KO) moved 0.85% higher in premarket action, while PepsiCo (PEP) slipped 0.15%.
